title: Tha Alaska Music Podcast - MARS
summary: The original soundtrack to the MARS multimedia exhibition at the Anchorage Museum - on display until September 7, 2026 - composed by Kurt Riemann, who is coincidentally the host of this show.

title: AST MICS 3, MIW 2: North Pole [AK25047292]
summary: UPDATE: After additional follow-up, Clay Kristiansen is being charged with MICS 3 and MIW 2. On 05/17/2025 at 2250 hours a Trooper conducted a traffic stop for a turn signal violation. The Trooper developed reasonable suspicion that the occupants of the vehicle were involved in narcotic activity. K9 Kenny was requested and responded to the scene. K9 Kenny had a positive indication on the vehicle. Ultimately, a search warrant was conducted and 56.5 grams of suspected methamphetamine, a scale, and a stolen handgun was seized from the vehicle. This investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: What You Can Do on the Kenai River in May (Even Without Salmon Fishing)
summary: Think there’s nothing to do on the Kenai River in May because the salmon haven’t arrived yet? Think again. title: Why You Should Book Your Alaska Fishing Trip in May (Even if You’re Fishing in July or August)
summary: You might not be fishing until July or August , but if you’re planning to fish the Kenai River this summer… May is when you book it. Why? title: May Fishing in Alaska – What to Expect on the Kenai & Kasilof Rivers
summary: Is May a good time to fish in Alaska? The answer: yes — if you know what to target. title: Federal Funding Cuts Halt Community Food Distributions
summary: Tlingit & Haida’s community food distributions for herring roe and salmon are on hold for 2025 due to federal funding cuts. title: Putting Indigenous Voices Back on the Map
summary: Photography By Schuyler Alig From mapping coastal erosion to preserving vital ecosystems, Geographic Information System (GIS) technology is giving Alaskan communities the power to protect their lands and ways of life.
